language: Albanian (Kosovo Gheg primary, standard for recognition)
source: CEFR A2 descriptors (functions layer per Core Inventory); standard Albanian reference grammar; Gheg forms verified against family usage by native-speaker teacher

cards.title: Simple past (aorist)
cards.goal: Can narrate completed past events with the simple past.
grammar: e kryera e thjeshtë: punova, shkova, pashë, hëngra, erdha — regular patterns + high-frequency irregulars
perfect vs aorist balance in Kosovo speech (calibrate to family)
cards.evidence: Tell yesterday in six sentences using at least four aorist forms correctly.

cards.title: Telling a story
cards.goal: Can tell a short connected story about a past experience.
grammar: mixing perfect and aorist naturally
discourse: sequencers: në fillim, pastaj/mandej, pas kësaj, në fund
cards.evidence: A 60-90 second true story with three sequencers; pass = listener can retell the events in order.

cards.title: How things were (imperfect)
cards.goal: Can describe past states, backgrounds and habits.
grammar: imperfect: isha, kisha, punoja, shkoja
kur isha e vogël… frame
cards.evidence: Five sentences contrasting then vs now, imperfect vs present correctly split.
skin_examples: her childhood in France vs life now

cards.title: The future, both ways
cards.goal: Can talk about future plans and intentions.
grammar: kam me + participle (Gheg): kam me shku nesër
do të + conjugated (standard): do të shkoj
question frames: Çka ke me bo? / Çka do të bësh?
cards.evidence: Describe next week: five future sentences plus one future question, consistent in whichever form the family favours.
cards.note: Already queued as the next lesson.

cards.title: Suggesting & arranging
cards.goal: Can make suggestions, invite, and arrange to meet.
grammar: hajde + me + participle / të + verb
exponents: Hajde me hangër / hajde të hamë
cards.evidence: Consensus roleplay: agree on a plan (what/when/where) making two suggestions and politely deflecting one.

cards.title: Little words that carry everything (object clitics)
cards.goal: Can use object pronouns (clitics) with common verbs.
grammar: pre-verbal clitics e, i, më, të, na, ju: E kam pa; A e njeh?; Më pëlqen; Ma jep
cards.evidence: Answer ten rapid questions whose answers require a clitic (A e do kafen? — Po, e du), 7/10 correct.
skin_examples: object-passing table talk — recycles SQ-A1-13 situations

cards.title: Whose & to whom (genitive and dative)
cards.goal: Can express possession and recipients: X's Y, to/for someone.
grammar: genitive with linking article: shtëpia e axhës, libri i vajzës
dative recipients: i thashë nanës, ia dhashë Durimit — high-frequency frames first, paradigm second
cards.evidence: Eight sentences: four X's-Y possessives across genders, four tell/give-to-someone frames.

cards.title: Plurals & amounts
cards.goal: Can use common plural forms and quantity words.
grammar: main plural patterns: -a, -e, -ë; definite plurals -t/-të; key irregulars
shumë, pak, ca, disa, sa + plural
cards.evidence: Produce plural (indefinite + definite) for ten familiar nouns, 8/10 correct, then four true quantity sentences.
skin_examples: family gatherings: shumë njerëz, pak karrige

cards.title: Comparing
grammar: më + adjective (+ se): më i madh se, më e mirë se
superlative: më i madhi / më e mira
agreement carried from SQ-A1-05
cards.evidence: Rank three items with two comparatives and one superlative, agreement correct.
skin_examples: whose byrek is best — deploy with care

cards.title: Asking & offering
cards.goal: Can make polite requests and offers and respond to them.
grammar: register: informal default; ju for elders per family norm
exponents: A mund me…? / A mundesh me…?
cards.evidence: Three-scene roleplay (ask for help, offer help, borrow something), appropriate register per scene.

cards.title: Reacting like a person, not a textbook
cards.goal: Can react in conversation with natural spoken markers.
discourse: Po, po; A thu?; Valla…; Shumë mirë!; S'ka problem; E di, e di; Bre (recognition only)
cards.evidence: During a five-minute chat, react appropriately eight times using at least four different markers.
skin_examples: reacting to Durim's side of a real family story

cards.title: Simple opinions
cards.goal: Can give a simple opinion and agree/disagree gently.
cards.evidence: React to six statements: three agreements, two soft disagreements, one s'e-di — each with one reason sentence.
skin_examples: food, films, France vs Kosovo — low-stakes topics only, by design

cards.title: Describing people & places
cards.goal: Can give a short connected description of a person or place.
grammar: consolidates SQ-A1-05 + SQ-A2-07/08/09
place frames: gjendet në…, ka shumë…, është e njohur për…
cards.evidence: A six-sentence spoken portrait of one family member (appearance + personality + one habit), recognisable to the listener.

cards.title: WhatsApp Albanian
cards.goal: Can hold a short written/voice exchange in the family's everyday register.
discourse: message conventions: flm, tung, si jeni?, shihemi, natën
lexis: real family messages (with permission) as authentic texts
cards.evidence: A three-turn WhatsApp exchange (text or voice) in Albanian only; pass = the exchange completes naturally.

cards.title: When it's too fast (repair kit)
cards.goal: Can keep a conversation alive when he/she doesn't understand, without switching to French or freezing.
cards.evidence: Deliberate-obstacle task: a 90-second slightly-too-fast story; reach full understanding using 3+ different repair moves, summarise back in French, give a one-sentence Albanian reaction.
skin_examples: any real family story — her hardest real-world moment, rehearsed safely
cards.note: Pull forward — do not wait for end of A2. The repair kit must exist before the first unrehearsed family conversation.
